tech_stack: Python, FastAPI, LangGraph, OpenAI SDK, Docker
repo_summary: AI-powered customer support system using a multi-agent LangGraph workflow with tool calling, session persistence, and a CI/CD pipeline. Approximately 3,200 lines across 30 files.

code_quality.evidence: Consistent type hints across all public functions. Ruff configured with strict rules. Clean separation: agents/, graph/, tools/, api/. Tests use pytest with fixtures. Minor gap: two functions exceed 40 lines in graph/router.py.
strengths: Full type annotations on all public interfaces
Ruff + mypy configured and passing in CI
Clean module boundaries with dependency injection
gaps: Two functions in graph/router.py exceed 40 lines

documentation.evidence: Candidate-authored README with architecture overview and Mermaid diagram. Module-level docstrings on all packages. Missing: individual function docstrings in tools/ module.
strengths: Architecture diagram in README using Mermaid
Module-level docstrings explain each package's responsibility
gaps: No function-level docstrings in tools/ module

conv_tooling.evidence: Docker multi-stage build with non-root user. GitHub Actions CI with lint, type-check, test, and build steps. Pre-commit hooks configured. Coverage threshold at 80%.
strengths: Multi-stage Docker build with health check

ai_tooling.evidence: LangGraph multi-agent workflow with 3 nodes. @tool decorator on 4 functions with bind_tools in agent nodes. Structured output via Pydantic state model. No RAG or eval framework.
strengths: Pydantic TypedDict for structured state management

learning.evidence: Met all challenge requirements. LangGraph is a reasonable framework choice but not novel. No bonus features beyond the spec.
gaps: No bonus features beyond spec requirements

shaping.evidence: specs/ directory with a single planning document outlining the approach. No numbered specs, no tasks.md, no requirements checklist.
gaps: Single spec file rather than phased numbered specs

ways_of_working.evidence: Feature branch with conventional commit messages. No PR templates, no CHANGELOG.
gaps: No PR templates or contribution guide

teaching.evidence: README has setup instructions and usage examples. No quickstart per feature, no recorded demos.
strengths: Clear setup and usage instructions in README
gaps: No docstring examples for complex functions

task_complexity.evidence: Multi-agent system with graph-based routing, tool calling, and session management. Moderate complexity relative to the brief.

ownership.evidence: All core requirements met. Error handling present but no retry logic or graceful degradation.

planning.evidence: Clean folder organization. Single planning spec present. No phased approach evidence.

business_impact.evidence: Customer support context reflected in prompts. No explicit cost/value analysis.

communication.evidence: Clear variable names, conventional commits, well-structured README.

company_match.evidence: No hardcoded secrets, non-root Docker user, CI pipeline enforces quality.

proactivity.evidence: Met requirements but no bonus features or self-initiated improvements.

engineering_practices.summary: Strong conventional tooling with full CI pipeline and Docker. Clean architecture with good test coverage. Gaps in observability and dependency management. Net adjustment: 0.
engineering_practices.adjustment_reason: All categories meet baseline expectations. No outstanding features or critical failures.

categories.name: Clean Architecture & Code Quality
categories.summary: Clean module separation, consistent patterns, minor long-function issues.

findings.label: Dependency injection in agent graph
findings.description: Agent nodes receive dependencies via graph state, not global imports.

findings.label: Long functions in router
findings.description: Two routing functions exceed 40 lines mixing validation and routing logic.
findings.suggestion: Extract validation into a separate function.

categories.name: Testing Practices
categories.summary: Unit tests with mocked LLM calls plus integration tests. 82% coverage.

findings.label: Mocked LLM responses
findings.description: All OpenAI calls mocked with realistic response fixtures.

categories.name: Static Code Analysis
categories.summary: Ruff + mypy configured and passing in CI with pre-commit hooks.

findings.label: Full static analysis suite
findings.description: Ruff linting, mypy type checking, and black formatting all configured.

categories.name: Pipeline & DevOps Quality
categories.summary: GitHub Actions CI with lint, type-check, test, build. Multi-stage Docker.

findings.label: Multi-stage Docker build
findings.description: Build stage separated from runtime. Non-root user. Health check defined.

categories.name: AI Framework Maturity
categories.summary: LangGraph multi-agent with tool calling, structured output, and prompt templates.

findings.label: LangGraph StateGraph workflow
findings.description: 3-node graph with conditional routing based on agent state.

categories.name: Security Vulnerabilities
categories.summary: No SAST/SCA tooling but no hardcoded secrets. Env vars for all config.

findings.label: No security scanning tooling
findings.description: No bandit, semgrep, or pip-audit configured.
findings.suggestion: Add bandit to pre-commit and pip-audit to CI.

categories.name: Dependency Management
categories.summary: Poetry with lock file. Dev/prod separation. No automated update tooling.

findings.label: Poetry with lock file
findings.description: Dependencies pinned via poetry.lock for reproducibility.

findings.label: No automated dependency updates
findings.suggestion: Add Dependabot for automated security updates.

categories.name: Observability & Monitoring
categories.summary: Python logging configured. No structured logs, no LLM call tracking, no health endpoint.

findings.label: No LLM observability
findings.description: No token usage or latency logging for OpenAI calls.
findings.suggestion: Add LangSmith or custom middleware to log LLM metrics.

categories.name: Error Handling & Resilience
categories.summary: Try/except blocks present. No retry logic or circuit breakers.

findings.label: No retry logic
findings.description: No exponential backoff on OpenAI API calls.
findings.suggestion: Add tenacity decorators with exponential backoff.

categories.name: Prompt Engineering Quality
categories.summary: Dedicated prompt files with role separation and scope boundaries.

findings.label: External prompt templates
findings.description: All prompts externalized with clear system/human role separation.

categories.name: API Design & Contract Quality
categories.summary: FastAPI with Pydantic validation, auto-generated OpenAPI, structured error responses.

findings.label: Structured error handling
findings.description: Custom exception handlers return structured JSON error responses.

categories.name: Configuration Management
categories.summary: Pydantic BaseSettings with .env.example and startup validation.

findings.label: Pydantic settings with validation
findings.description: BaseSettings class fails fast on missing required environment variables.

categories.name: Code Complexity
categories.summary: No files exceed 300 lines. Two functions exceed 40 lines but none exceed 60.

findings.label: Controlled file sizes
findings.description: Largest file is 280 lines. Good decomposition across modules.
